stimulus: A recent study followed the number of pollinating moth species visiting twenty-four high-elevation meadows in the Colorado Rockies over an eight-year period. ______blank reported that when nearby coniferous edges were thinned for fuel management, the diversity of moths visiting the meadows fell sharply within two seasons.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. "Lepidopterist" is an indefinite role, so the proper name "Mei Watanabe" supplies essential identification and is restrictive—no commas. No punctuation should separate the subject from its verb "reported."

Choice A is incorrect because a colon does not correctly introduce a finite verb. Choice B is incorrect because a comma between subject and verb is ungrammatical. Choice C is incorrect because it wrongly treats the name as nonrestrictive supplementary information.
